purpose: Step-by-step guide for deploying Idle Kingdom Revolution from local development to production.
status_note: v0.2.0 reconciles this guide with the actual Phase 0/Phase 1 implementation (bun instead of npm, no Playwright/MSW/shadcn yet, actual CI workflow). Phase 1-2 sections below are historical record of initial setup steps already completed; Phase 3+ reflects current real tooling. Rollback procedure added per Phase 1 follow-up.

local_tools: Bun (actual package manager in use — see bun.lock)
VS Code (recommended) with extensions: ESLint, Prettier, Tailwind CSS IntelliSense

phase_1_project_setup.status: COMPLETED (Phase 0) — kept here as historical record

step_3_init_nextjs.options: Actual project builds with --webpack flag (next build --webpack); Turbopack default was not used due to broken SWC native binary on the original Windows dev machine.

step_4_install_dependencies.note: Original plan listed npm + framer-motion + auth-helpers-nextjs + testing-library + playwright + msw. Actual installed set is the bun.lock dependency list — narrower than originally planned. See package.json for current source of truth.

step_5_install_shadcn.status: NOT DONE — shadcn/ui is not present in package.json or components.json usage yet. Revisit if/when a component library is adopted.

folders: src/app/(game)/ — EXISTS BUT EMPTY, dead route group. Either populate it or delete it; real game pages currently live under src/app/game/ without a route group.
src/app/admin/ — NOT YET CREATED (admin panel is Phase 1 backlog item P1-13)
src/app/api/ — DONE, partially populated (see 21_IMPLEMENTATION_CHECKLIST / phase-1/completed.md for which routes still lack route.ts)
tests/unit/ — DONE (added alongside this deployment guide update; see tests/unit/game/)
tests/e2e/ — NOT YET CREATED. No Playwright in the project yet; 07_TESTING_STRATEGY.json calls for it but it has not been installed.

step_1_create_project.action: Go to supabase.com, create new project
step_1_create_project.settings: Region: closest to your users (eu-central-1 for Europe), Password: strong generated password

copy: service_role key (for admin, NOT currently used anywhere in src/ — keep it out of client code if/when admin panel is built)

step_3_env_local.note: SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY should only be added to .env.local once an admin-only server-side client is actually implemented. Never prefix it with NEXT_PUBLIC_.

step_4_run_schema.action: Open Supabase SQL Editor, paste contents of supabase/migrations/ in order
step_4_run_schema.note: Run in order: 001_initial_schema.sql -> 002_auth_triggers.sql -> 003 (game tables) migration. See phase-1/completed.md for the exact migration history and known schema fixes already applied.

configure: Google OAuth — NOT YET ENABLED (backlog item, intentionally deferred)
Anonymous sign-in — ENABLED (guest mode uses supabase.auth.signInAnonymously)

step_6_seed_data.content: 12 units, 11 items, 5 fields, plus other seed datasets per phase-0/completed.md

files: src/proxy.ts (route protection, replaces middleware.ts under Next.js 16)
src/stores/ — EXISTS, verify gameStore/uiStore coverage matches actual usage

step_3_core_screens.status: Pages exist for: battle, army, mining, td, castle, inventory, leaderboard, mail, quests, shop, valor. Verify each against its required API route (some are missing — see step_4 below).

step_4_api_routes.status: PARTIAL — known gaps as of this audit
step_4_api_routes.note: Several frontend pages (army, castle, td) already render and likely call these missing endpoints. Treat this list as the immediate next backend task, not just a future nice-to-have.

step_5_testing.unit: bun run test  (Vitest — added 2026-06-24, covers src/lib/game/*)
step_5_testing.e2e: NOT YET SET UP — Playwright is specified in 07_TESTING_STRATEGY.json but not installed. Add when Phase 1 critical paths (register -> battle -> save) are stable enough to be worth locking down with E2E.
step_5_testing.coverage: bunx vitest run --coverage (requires @vitest/coverage-v8, not yet installed)

step_1_github_actions.status: DONE — updated 2026-06-24 to add typecheck (verified clean via `npm run typecheck` on 2026-06-24, now a blocking gate), unit tests (blocking), and non-blocking dependency audit, in addition to the existing file-presence check and webpack build.
step_1_github_actions.note: The CI workflow is bun-based (oven-sh/setup-bun), not npm-based — this guide previously assumed npm and was out of date.

settings: Publish directory: .next (SSR via Next.js runtime, not static export)
Deploy previews: Enable for pull requests

step_3_environment_variables.warning: Never add SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY or SUPABASE_PASSWORD to Netlify env vars unless an admin-only server context actually needs them, and even then double-check it's never exposed as NEXT_PUBLIC_*.

step_4_custom_domain.action: Domain settings > Add custom domain (not yet done — currently on idle-kingdom-revolution.netlify.app)
step_4_custom_domain.note: Update Supabase Auth redirect URLs to include custom domain when this happens

checklist: manifest.json is valid — DONE (public/manifest.json exists per phase-0/completed.md)
Service worker registers — VERIFY, not confirmed in this audit
Icons present in all sizes — VERIFY
Install prompt appears on mobile — VERIFY

monitoring.sentry: NOT YET SET UP — optional, free tier available, consider before any real user traffic
monitoring.lighthouse: Not yet wired into CI despite 07_TESTING_STRATEGY.json mentioning a >80/>90 budget. Add as a CI step (e.g. treosh/lighthouse-ci-action) once there's a stable deployed preview URL to test against.

backup_strategy.database: Supabase > Database > Backups (daily automated on most plans — verify current plan tier)
backup_strategy.manual_backup: pg_dump via Supabase CLI weekly (not yet scheduled/automated — manual reminder for now)

rollback_procedure.purpose: What to do when a deploy to production breaks something, added per Phase 1 follow-up request — this was missing entirely from the original guide.

frontend_rollback.netlify_instant_rollback: Netlify keeps every previous deploy. Go to Site > Deploys, find the last known-good deploy, click 'Publish deploy' (or 'Rollback' depending on UI version) to instantly repoint production traffic to it. This does NOT touch the database — it only changes which built frontend bundle is served.
frontend_rollback.git_revert: If the bad deploy came from a merged PR: git revert <merge-commit-sha> on main, push, let CI redeploy. Prefer this over force-pushing history rewrites on a shared branch.
frontend_rollback.when_to_use_which: Netlify instant rollback = fastest, use first to stop the bleeding. git revert = proper fix afterward so main reflects reality and the next deploy doesn't reintroduce the bug.

database_rollback.migration_rollback: Supabase migrations in this project are forward-only SQL files (001, 002, 003...). There is currently no down-migration / rollback script per migration. Before running a new migration against production, take a manual snapshot (see manual_backup above) or use Supabase's point-in-time recovery if on a paid plan.
database_rollback.point_in_time_recovery: Supabase Pro+ plans support PITR (point-in-time recovery) — restores the whole database to a specific timestamp. This is the safety net for a bad migration, not a per-table undo. Confirm current plan tier supports this before relying on it.
risky_migration_checklist: Does this migration drop or rename a column/table that existing code reads from?
Has it been tested against a local/staging Supabase instance first?
Is there a recent backup or PITR window to fall back to if it goes wrong?
Can the API routes that depend on the changed schema be deployed in the same release, or do they need to ship together?

general_incident_steps: 1. Roll back the frontend first via Netlify (fastest mitigation, buys time).
2. Check Supabase logs + Netlify function/build logs to identify root cause.
3. If the issue is data corruption (not just bad code), stop — do not run further migrations until you've decided whether PITR or manual fix-up SQL is the right tool.
4. Fix forward via a new PR once the cause is understood; avoid re-deploying the same broken commit.
5. After resolution, add a regression test (unit or E2E) covering what broke, so CI catches it next time.

update_workflow.development: bun run dev, test locally with bun run test
update_workflow.pr: Create pull request, CI runs check + typecheck + tests + build
update_workflow.merge: Merge to main, auto-deploy to Netlify
update_workflow.verify: Check production, monitor errors, keep rollback_procedure above in mind if something looks wrong

troubleshooting.build_fails: Check Node version (20+), clear node_modules, bun install --frozen-lockfile
troubleshooting.swc_binary_broken_on_windows: Known issue on this project's original Windows dev machine — use `next build --webpack` / `next dev` may need the same flag if Turbopack errors locally. @next/swc-win32-x64-msvc is in dependencies; if this is still failing, try removing node_modules and reinstalling before assuming it's a code issue.
troubleshooting.auth_not_working: Verify redirect URLs in Supabase match your domain exactly
troubleshooting.rls_errors: Check RLS policies, ensure user is authenticated
troubleshooting.cors_errors: Add your domain to Supabase CORS origins
troubleshooting.database_connection_limit: Supabase free = 60 connections. Use connection pooling for serverless.
troubleshooting.netlify_build_timeout: Build taking >15 min? Check for infinite loops in static generation.
troubleshooting.vitest_path_alias_not_resolving: vitest.config.ts must declare resolve.alias for '@' pointing at ./src to match tsconfig.json's paths — Vitest does not read tsconfig paths automatically without a plugin like vite-tsconfig-paths. Fixed 2026-06-24.
